rationale: A document-level critical red flag fired (rule M1): the auditor expressed substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern, and management disclosed a material weakness in internal control over financial reporting. Under the deterministic matrix these are automatic critical-review conditions and require no financial metrics to trigger, because they speak to whether the reported numbers can be trusted at all.
counter_evidence: A going-concern paragraph is a disclosure about risk, not a bankruptcy filing; companies do sometimes raise capital or restructure and recover. If this is held as a small position already sized for the possibility of a total loss, some of the risk may be reflected in where it trades.
what_would_change_this: A later filing in which the auditor removes the going-concern qualification
Auditor-confirmed remediation of the disclosed material weakness
A financing or restructuring that removes near-term solvency risk
disclaimer: Educational analysis of public information for the portfolio owner's own decision-making. Not individualized investment advice. Data may be incomplete or delayed.
